police.name: Chesterfield County Police
police.meta: Chesterfield County · figures from the FY2026 county budget and the department's 2024 annual report
police.lead: Who runs the Chesterfield County Police Department, where it is, what it costs, how many officers it has, and what its officers did last year. Every figure links back to an official source so you can check it yourself.

chief.title: Chief of Police
chief.name: Col. Edward F. "Frank" Carpenter Jr.
chief.note: Carpenter leads the department through the Office of the Chief and three branches: Operations, Administrative Services, and Professional Standards. The department serves about 387,700 residents across 437 square miles.

police.budget_note: The county budgets the Police Department together with Animal Services, the 911 Emergency Communications Center, and the Magistrate's Office, so this $119.3M line is not police-only. The department reported its police operations at about $99.2 million in fiscal 2025.
police.staffing_note: Authorized strength in 2024 was 564 sworn officers, plus 153 full-time and 68 part-time professional (civilian) staff. More than 200 volunteers contributed over 23,000 hours in 2024.

stations.note: Chesterfield Police operate from a central headquarters at the County complex on Iron Bridge Road, supported by community-policing offices and patrol facilities around the county rather than numbered precincts.

police.work_dek: From the department's 2024 annual report, the most recent full year it has published; a police annual report comes out months after the year ends, so the 2025 edition is not out yet. The department does not publish a single annual total-arrests figure, so the enforcement numbers below are the ones it reports.
police.current_note: More recent, partial 2025 data: through November 2025, overall reported crime was down to about 12,453 offenses from 14,113 in 2024, though domestic-violence, weapons, and drug-possession cases rose, per a county crime report. The full 2025 annual report, with updated arrest and traffic figures, will update this page when it is released.

fire.name: Chesterfield Fire & EMS
fire.meta: Chesterfield County · figures from the FY2026 county budget and the 2026 Community Risk Assessment & Standards of Cover
fire.lead: Who runs Chesterfield Fire & EMS, where its stations are, what it costs, how many firefighters and medics it has, and how many emergencies it answers. Every figure links back to an official source.

chief.title: Fire Chief
chief.name: Edward L. "Loy" Senter Jr.
chief.note: Chesterfield Fire & EMS is a career department supplemented by volunteers, covering 437 square miles and about 387,700 residents. It grew out of the unification of community volunteer fire companies decades ago and holds an ISO Class 2 rating.

fire.budget_note: Beyond the General Fund total shown, a separate grants fund of about $9.7 million (mostly EMS billing / revenue recovery) also supports the department. General Fund spending grew 6.4% over FY2025.
fire.staffing_note: For fiscal 2026 the department has 605 authorized positions. Operationally that is about 562 career firefighters and 53 civilian professionals, backed by 56 volunteer firefighters and partnerships with volunteer rescue squads.
fire.work_dek: From the 2026 Community Risk Assessment & Standards of Cover. About four of every five calls are medical (EMS), not fire.

stations.name: Manchester
stations.address: 7541 Hull Street Road, North Chesterfield, VA 23235

stations.name: Dale
stations.address: 5811 Iron Bridge Road, North Chesterfield, VA 23234

fire.stations_note: 21 career fire stations, plus 2 volunteer fire stations and 7 volunteer EMS rescue-squad stations across the county.

sources.label: 2026 Community Risk Assessment & Standards of Cover
